title: Beaconing and C2 Detection
story: Network behavioral analytics flagged a workstation making highly periodic
outbound connections to the same external IP address.
Malware implants commonly 'beacon' — check in with C2 servers at regular
intervals to receive commands and report status. The regularity of the
intervals is the key detection signal.
Analyze the network logs. Find the infected host, the C2 server,
and determine the precise beacon interval.

objectives.label: Identify the C2 server IP address
What is the beacon interval in seconds?
What port does the C2 communicate on?

hints: Look for periodic connections: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case9_firewall | stats count by src_ip, dest_ip | sort -count
Beaconing detection: same src/dst pair with very consistent time deltas
Use timechart: ... | timechart count by dest_ip span=1m — look for regular spikes
splunk_queries: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case9_firewall | stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port | sort -count
index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case9_firewall src_ip=10.0.1.50 | timechart count by dest_ip span=5m
index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case9_suricata | table _time src_ip dest_ip signature
